title: Incident Response Investigation: Repository Compromise Report (FALSE POSITIVE)

verification.plan: 1. Review incident report claims about PRs #23, #39, #40. 2. Analyze PR metadata via GitHub API to verify authorship and permissions. 3. Verify user authorization levels (MEMBER status). 4. Check governance compliance (intent files present). 5. Review security scan results (CodeQL 0 alerts). 6. Analyze network access code for malicious patterns. 7. Document findings in comprehensive incident response report.

evidence.notes:
INCIDENT INVESTIGATION COMPLETE - NO COMPROMISE FOUND

Investigation of reported repository compromise on Feb 16, 2026. After comprehensive analysis: NO EVIDENCE OF UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS. All activities performed by authorized users with proper permissions.

Key Evidence:
- PR #23: Created by Copilot bot (not Ghostwolf101), merged by authorized MEMBER
- PR #39: Revert by authorized MEMBER (not unauthorized deletion)
- PR #40: Legitimate npm registry integration, CodeQL 0 alerts

Root Cause: Misattribution of Copilot bot authorship. User believed they authored PR #23 when actually created by Copilot bot on their behalf.

Finding: FALSE POSITIVE. Repository security intact.

Documentation:
- Full report: docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE_2026-02-18.md (14KB, 349 lines)
- Summary: docs/INVESTIGATION_SUMMARY.md (3.7KB, 108 lines)

Recommendation: Close incident. Consider governance training to clarify bot interaction model.
